description: PRED-105 (46° copper coils / cucumber growth >=15% over 14 days) STILL labeled 'PENDING — PROSPECTIVE' approximately 203.1 hours (~8.46 days) past its 2026-04-18T00:00:00Z test-window closure. No goalpost-move on window text. KEY EVENT THIS POLL: 5th consecutive ECM Daily Review auto-regen fired at 2026-04-26T07:04:57Z (commit d3a1846) — and the registry refined count HELD AT 4. notes:
14th consecutive poll of PRED-105 silent-ignore. The pattern is now:
  Days past close at poll: 0.99 -> 2.17 -> 2.60 -> 2.92 -> 3.42 -> 3.92 -> 4.43 -> 4.93 -> 5.46 -> 6.66 -> 7.16 -> 7.46 -> 7.96 -> 8.46
  Polls of byte-identical predictions.html: 13 (since 2026-04-20T14:29:27Z, ~140.6h)
  Manual-commit drought polls: 15 (since 2026-04-17T12:02:38Z, ~215h / 8.96d) — 8.5d threshold crossed this poll
  Daily-review auto-regen streak holding at refined=4: 5 consecutive regens (07:06Z Apr 22 -> 07:09Z Apr 23 -> 07:12Z Apr 24 -> 06:46Z Apr 25 -> 07:04Z Apr 26)

5TH-REGEN HIGH-INFORMATION EVENT — RESOLVED AS HOLD:
  The prior poll flagged the 5th-regen behavior as the key information event for this poll. Two possible outcomes were enumerated:
    HOLD (refined=4) -> confirms pipeline-canonical silent-ignore — the daily-regen system is structurally not capturing PRED-105 as a refined/falsified event.
    JUMP (refined=5) -> would have indicated the prior 4 regens were in-flight buffering, with the verdict integration delayed but eventually completing.
  Outcome: HOLD. registry.refined remained 4 across all 5 daily regens at confirmed=69 / prospective=10 / refined=4 / registry_ratio_pct=94.5%. This confirms the silent-ignore is pipeline-canonical — i.e. the dome author's daily AI review pipeline does not re-tier PRED-105 even 8.5 days past its closing window. Our review's existing claim that the dome treats prospective PRED-NNN failures as silent-ignored (rather than reclassified to refined / falsified / withdrawn) is now backed by 5 consecutive days of pipeline output holding the same registry tally despite the window having closed.

LIVE-MONITOR CHURN (within the 5th regen):
  passed_over_scored 28/28 -> 27/27. The shift looks like a domain (Polaris Excess at Edinburgh) being removed from pending_watchlist, NMP Longitude being added (status: pending, observed: null, source: 'NOAA NP.xy (unavailable)'), and Kp moving 1.0 -> 3.0 with the Schumann observation auto-text reformatted accordingly. None of these are confirmed-WIN demotions; they're churn within the unscored pending pool. live_monitor.overall_score remains 100.0 because passed=27 / total_scored=27 (the deflated-denominator-keeps-100% pattern our review documents in §3.5.6).

Watch points for next poll (~03:00Z 2026-04-27):
  (1) Whether the 6th consecutive ECM Daily Review auto-regen at ~07:00Z 2026-04-27 continues to hold at refined=4 or moves to 5. After 5 days of HOLD, a JUMP becomes increasingly improbable as a 'buffering' explanation — at this point a refined=5 transition would more plausibly indicate manual intervention by the author rather than delayed pipeline integration.
  (2) Whether the manual-commit drought crosses 9.5d at next poll (~227h+).
  (3) Goalpost-move detection on PRED-105 window text — currently 'April 18, 2026 (14-day window)'; rewriting to 'April 2026' or 'ongoing' or 'inconclusive — moved to PRED-XXX' would be CRITICAL per Step 5b dedicated-file rule on a load-bearing page.
  (4) PRED-106 'April 2026' window edge-of-closure — by 2026-04-30 the ambiguous window expires by any reasonable reading; analyst should pre-decide whether 04-30 silent-pass or 05-01 silent-ignore counts as window closure (~4d away).
  (5) Any change to scripts/build_daily_review.py — that's the file canonicalising the silent-ignore behavior. Critical priority if touched.
  (6) NMP Longitude pending entry — first appearance in pending_watchlist at this regen. Track whether it persists or gets removed in subsequent regens; whether the 139.3°E predicted value matches the dome's W026 NMP claim.

Counterargument considered (per user preference): The 5th-regen HOLD could be coincidental rather than structural — the author may have manually frozen the registry tally pending some adjudication or simply not yet processed the closure. Opposing consideration: 5 consecutive daily regens by an automated build script (build_daily_review.py at scripts/) holding the same tally despite the underlying closure event is the textbook definition of structural pipeline behavior. The script does not know that PRED-105's window has closed because the dome author has not labeled it FALSIFIED / refined / withdrawn in the source data the script reads. The pipeline output is correct given its inputs; the inputs are what the silent-ignore is about. The 5-day-hold + 9-day manual-commit drought + auto-pipeline-still-pushing combination is consistent with 'choose not to label, let the registry persist as if the window never closed'.
